title: Understand: Executive Branch—The Founders' Vision
description: Check your understanding of the Founders' vision of the Executive Branch with this set of multiple-choice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Federalist No. 68, what is the primary purpose of the Electoral College? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To ensure equal representation for all states in presidential elections
To prevent the election of a tyrant or demagogue
To give the House of Representatives exclusive power over presidential elections
To allow for direct popular election of the president

assessment_items.question_content:
**In his discussion of the executive branch in Federalist No. 70, Alexander Hamilton argues for which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A group of executives, to prevent abuse of power
A single, energetic executive to provide effective and decisive leadership
A weak executive to limit government overreach and prevent tyranny
A parliamentary system with a ceremonial head of state

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Federalist No. 68, who would serve as electors in the Electoral College?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: State governors
Members of the Senate
Specially chosen citizens
All voters in each state

assessment_items.question_content:
**What role did George Washington play in the Constitutional Convention of 1787? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He served as the primary author of the Constitution.
He presided over the Convention as its president.
He boycotted the Convention to protest its proceedings.
He led a group calling for a return to monarchy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did George Washington's actions as the first president influence the development of the presidency? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He established the expectation that presidents would serve only one term in office.
He expanded the powers of the presidency beyond the powers and duties set out in Article II of the Constitution.
He set norms for presidential behavior and the exercise of executive authority.
He pushed to serve more than two terms in office.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In Federalist No. 70, why does Alexander Hamilton argue  for a single executive? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To prevent the emergence of a strong executive who could threaten liberty
To create a system of checks and balances within the executive branch
To ensure that the president could act decisively and efficiently in times of crisis
To allow for the rotation of power among different branches of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did George Washington's leadership during the Revolutionary War influence his role in shaping the presidency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It made him desire a strong executive capable of decisive action.
It led him to advocate for a weak executive to prevent the abuse of power.
It caused him to call for a decentralized form of government.
It had no significant impact on his views on the presidency.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What principle regarding the presidency did Federalist No. 70 emphasize? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The importance of executive accountability to the legislature
The necessity of a strong executive to preserve national security
The need for a separation of powers within the executive branch
The danger of giving too much power to a single executive

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which aspects of the presidency are discussed in Article II?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The steps in the process for impeaching a president
How to elect a president to office
The different executive agencies under the president
The powers and responsibilities of the president
How many terms a president can serve in office

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which framer most influenced and shaped the vision of the presidency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Benjamin Franklin
James Madison
Abraham Lincoln
George Washington

assessment_items.question_content:
**George Washington played a significant role in shaping the presidency. What are THREE precedents Washington set during his time as president?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president should serve only two terms.
The president has the power to remove executive officers.
The president must give a State of the Union address.
The president should play a role in foreign affairs.
The president should choose a vice president from their own political party.

assessment_items.question_content:
**A key debate at the Constitutional Convention was about what role should the president play in the lawmaking process. Which statement best summarizes the compromise the framers reached?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The president has an absolute veto over congressional legislation and Congress has no power to override it.
The president, with members of the federal judiciary,  has the power to review legislation and decide to veto it.
The president has the power to comment on legislation but no power to veto any law made by Congress.
The president can veto congressional legislation, but Congress has the power to override any veto.
